author: @hsieh.kimber
text:
Dear Robert, 
My name is Chiachun Hsieh, and you can also call me Kimber, from ESCP Sustainability Entrepreneurship and Innovation. I am currently working on a project about rare earth elements and energy use in Africa.  Would you mind me asking some questions?
Thank you in advance!

author: @worldcomicsreview354
text: Don't fan motors need permanent magnets adding before they can function as generators? Or do certain types "just work"? I saw a video by some perpetual motion swindler saying you can turn a floor fan into a waterwheel that will power an incandescent bulb and a drill. Somebody in the comments said it would only make 20 watts, but that's still a decent bit of juice to slowly charge a power station for a rainy day.

author: @cimota72
text:
Hi Robert

Another inspiring video. 

How do you reckon we would size a home made turbine? I mean if we build the turbine itself, how do we size the brushless DC motor we would put into it?

author: @ryanlebeck259
text: You do great work recovering materials for thermal, solar, hydro, and wind. Have you given much thought on the University of South Hampton piezoelectric materials research on sound as a means of generation and salvaging materials for that kind of thing?

author: @crawfish069
text: How is your battery going? Do you have an update for us? Last time I heard you had to get the battery checked and tested by a third party.

author: @crawfish069
text: That fan would cost at least $9usd in America, even at the discount store. That's the lowest price I could find that for at a brick and mortar store. But our gasoline is cheaper at about $2usd per gallon.

author: @ssgeek4515
text: There's no better way than actually doing it with tools. Unfortunately schools want it down on paper but if you had a bad memory you forget your revision so yes I did poorly at school...but I can repair anything ....judge me now!

author: @nonyabusiness9747
text: I have this fan, and live off grid. Would of been cool if you should installing the rectophyer..

replies.author: @Gorguruga
replies.text: How much electricity can you generate with the fan?

author: @izudeenadegoke1067
text: Pls can you assist me how to construct xenon lamp power supply with a pulse function that can power up to 1000W. Kindly help with the circuit diagram. I am currently working on the project as an undergraduate at Fountain University,Osogbo, Osun, Nigeria.

author: @urbixs
text:
Idea is OK. But how practical it is?
I guuess it is about 40 cm in diametre then it could harvest about 5W at 6m/s wind. Could be used for emergency phone charger if proper electronic circuit added.
We always must look on the practical usage of these interesting and sometimes weird ideas :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the idea is to experiment - I have no idea how practical that is - I guess that's up to the experimenter to make it practical - so I have to disagree - practical is not always the point but it should be the goal for sure

author: @Justin-bd2dg
text: A brushed, DC, electric motor, being turned mechanically, is a generator, generators produce DC power absent rectification. Alternators produce AC and would need rectifying to produce DC. You adding a rectifier is a waste and improper.

author: @stevemyers2092
text: thank you - interesting

author: @danielade9694
text: Or how about a joule Thief circuit to up the voltage

author: @danielade9694
text: What a voltage multiplier work to up the voltage a little bit from 1 Volt or so that would be an interesting test to see

author: @EnterTheRealm
text: The energy generated from a brushed dc motor is not AC and doesn't need to be rectified.

replies.author: @mikeg4972
replies.text:
Agree.
Why add the 1.2V drop when you don't have to?

replies.author: @nonyabusiness9747
replies.text: Wait so.. does that mean one could connect the two red and black wires directly into a 12v battery to charge it??

replies.author: @EnterTheRealm
replies.text: @nonyabusiness9747 No, that motor wont produce 12v maybe if it was a universal motor and you activated the field windings to act as an electromagnet. Also you would need atleast one diode to stop the battery powering the motor.

author: @Limbiclesion
text: I really like your channel...As a compliment ....it’s like spending a sabbatical at Hogwarts ...😀

author: @stevetobias4890
text: Thanks Rob, I agree that theory only gets you so far. I have learnt loads from experiments and failure.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I think it is how we learn mate best mate

author: @elizabethmckinney7182
text: If you wanted to combine multiple sources, how would you do that? Say I made several wind generators from different sized motors. They'd have different outputs. How do I combine them into one power source?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: yeah I must do a video on that

author: @juggernautsvaderdmunchkinland
text: Thanks Robert!

author: @soufianebellahbib3837
text: I AM TEACHING🤓

author: @colouroboros9993
text: Thank you Rob

author: @garyhardman8369
text:
I was not going to comment on your power generation  videos, but feel that I must raise one pertinent issue.
You disagree with people questioning the efficiency  of the machines that you feature.
I believe  that  the commenters  have confused efficiency with  usefulness .
How much  a machine that generates one Watt is neither use nor ornament.
Would you festoon your house with hundreds of units that provided a few hundred watts?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I don't disagree mate - I think it is a good question in some respects - but it does miss the point of the videos - some are good machines - some are just experiments - but all are about trying something rather than trying nothing - it's because I believe it is by doing that we learn.

author: @martynjones973
text: Lovely stuff 👍

author: @thornhedge9639
text:
I thought an electric fan was a wind generator by the very nature of what it is...LOL!
    Always fun Buddy; Thanks again...

author: @AM-us7cz
text: Thanks again Rob, from the smales to the largest, you never stop inspiring me. Cheers Mate 🍻

author: @pulesjet
text: Short and sweat.

author: @jonflannery8984
text: If or when the stuff ever hits the fan you are definitely welcome to come kick it at my house. Thanks for more awesome content and trying to get more folks to see the treasures and potential in what would otherwise be rubbish. The world needs a lot more of this type of repurpose and up cycle thinking.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: absolutely agree mate - and thanks for the offer - cheers

author: @jonflannery8984
text: First. Woot.
